main_remaining_risk: The Level-2 mild_vs_strong boundary is the hardest to draw (75% confusion in §V.D audit). If gold adjudication reveals mild_vs_strong κ < 0.40, the paper must fall back to Level-1 three-class + binary strong_action screening per §VII.K. The hierarchical framing reduces but does not eliminate this risk; it converts a fatal 'forced four-class' attack into a pre-registered graceful-degradation path. A secondary residual risk is that the RIGOURATE-style scalar baseline (§VI.E) is a proxy, not the official RIGOURATE system; conclusions may shift when official artifacts become available.
next_required_action: Run the §VII 50-pair two-layer gold pilot (Layer 1 relation label + Layer 2 realism) using the frozen gold_pilot_protocol_freeze_v1 package and the v2 relation+realism protocol. Specifically: (1) execute blind annotation A and B; (2) adjudicate disagreements; (3) compute Level-1 κ (supported/overclaim/contradiction) and Level-2 κ (scope_expansion vs. strong_action conditional on overclaim); (4) compute strong_action retention rate; (5) apply the §VII.K fallback rules: if Level-1 κ ≥ 0.50 and Level-2 κ ≥ 0.40 and retention ≥ 0.60, retain the hierarchical taxonomy; if Level-1 κ ≥ 0.50 but Level-2 κ < 0.40, fall back to Level-1 three-class + binary strong_action screening; if retention < 0.50, downgrade strong_action to exploratory. Insert gold results into Tables G1–G3 without restructuring the paper.
audit_disclaimer: V3.15 hierarchical taxonomy revision. Wording-level and interpretive-level revision only. No experiments run, no API calls, no gold annotation, no original data modified, no experimental numbers changed. Four operational labels preserved as experimental label space; only interpretation changes from flat four-class to hierarchical. mild_scope_overclaim explicitly NOT claimed to be naturally stable. R4 explicitly NOT claimed to overall beat LLM (R4 macro-F1 0.3280 < LLM 0.5523 on matched 100). All silver labels remain silver. Three-class fallback is pre-registered, NOT executed. Gold is planned (§VII), NOT complete.
